Author: XohanTheTrader
Reflection:
• Workload is fine, it's the same as all other maths modules. This one has an extra problem sheet that's due in week 11, which is wacky and annoying, plus you can't drop one of the sheets, so it's kinda got the highest load of all the non-coursework modules.
• If you keep up with the module, you'll be fine when exams come.

• Problem sheets are badly written, but doable.
• The problem sheets weren't released to the seminar tutors until very late, so they weren't marked very quickly.

• Lectures were fairly slow, and the lecturer can spend a long time on examples.
• The lecture notes are often released after the lectures, which is bad if you have reasonable adjustments. If they're yet again being given out late, take it up with your SSLC reps, and they can fix that. Resources in general aren't very helpful. Slideshows are slightly humorous when he shows rabbits :>

• Half of the module is recurrence relations, and the other half is pretty much all covered by other core DM modules. The questions are long but not hard.
• No prior knowledge required, plus most things are redundant for other core and optional modules.
• Did not enjoy it. "If you do this module you will heavily dislike it"
• This is good for people who have interests in graph theory and other discrete maths-y topics, but it can't be taken as an option, so this advice is pretty pointless.
• Tip: read a textbook rather than lecture notes, and if you want the full lecture notes just ask someone in an older year.
